guide.title: User Guide

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.desc: Get up and running in 5 minutes — clone, build, and launch

prerequisites.title: 1. Clone repo & check Go version
prerequisites.desc: Run git clone https://github.com/chef-guo/agents-hive.git && cd agents-hive to clone the project. Then run go version to confirm version ≥ 1.24.
prerequisites.tip: If Go version is below 1.24, download the latest from https://golang.org/dl/.
prerequisites.detail:
## Clone the Project

```bash
git clone https://github.com/chef-guo/agents-hive.git
cd agents-hive
```

## Check Go Version

```bash
go version
# Expected output: go version go1.24.x linux/amd64 (or darwin/arm64, etc.)
```

> Go version must be ≥ 1.24. If below, download the latest from [golang.org/dl](https://golang.org/dl/).

## Check Node.js Version (for frontend development)

```bash
node -v
# Expected: v18.x.x or higher
npm -v
# Expected: 9.x.x or higher
```

> Node.js is only needed for local frontend development. In production, the frontend is embedded in the Go binary.

buildAndRun.title: 2. Build CLI and Server
buildAndRun.desc:
Run the following commands to build:
• go build -o claw ./cmd/claw — build the CLI client
• go build -o server ./cmd/server — build the HTTP server
Then set your API key: export OPENAI_API_KEY="your-key"
buildAndRun.tip: You can also use CLAW_API_KEY env var (higher priority than OPENAI_API_KEY). Supports OpenAI / DeepSeek / Anthropic and more.
buildAndRun.detail:
## Build the Project

The project has two executables. Build them separately:

### Build CLI Client

```bash
go build -o claw ./cmd/claw
```

### Build HTTP Server

```bash
go build -o server ./cmd/server
```

Verify both executables were created:

```bash
ls -la claw server
# -rwxr-xr-x  1 user  staff  12345678  claw
# -rwxr-xr-x  1 user  staff  23456789  server
```

## Set API Key

Choose one method to set the LLM API key:

**Option 1: Environment Variable (Recommended)**

```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-your-api-key-here"
```

**Option 2: CLAW-specific Variable** (higher priority)

```bash
export CLAW_API_KEY="sk-your-api-key-here"
```

> Supports OpenAI, DeepSeek, Anthropic, and more. When switching providers, also set `CLAW_PROVIDER` and `CLAW_BASE_URL`.

verify.title: 3. Start the service and verify
verify.desc:
CLI mode: ./claw -i to start interactive session, type "hello" to test.
Server mode: ./server --port 8080 to start backend, then cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev to start the frontend. Visit http://localhost:3000 — seeing the dashboard means success.
verify.tip: In dev mode, the frontend auto-proxies /api requests to localhost:8080. In production, the frontend is embedded in the Go binary — just access port 8080 directly.
verify.detail:
## CLI Mode Verification

Start an interactive session:

```bash
./claw -i
```

Type "hello" to test the agent:

```
> hello
🤔 Thinking...
✅ Hello! I'm the CLAW agent. How can I help you?
```

Press `Ctrl+C` to exit.

## Server Mode Verification

### 1. Start the Backend

```bash
./server --port 8080
# Expected output:
# INFO  Server started successfully  {"port": 8080}
```

### 2. Start the Frontend Dev Server

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
# Expected output:
#   VITE v8.x.x  ready in xxx ms
#   ➜  Local:   http://localhost:3000/
```

### 3. Verify

Open `http://localhost:3000` in your browser. Seeing the dashboard means setup is complete!

> In dev mode, the frontend auto-proxies `/api` requests to `localhost:8080`. In production, the frontend is embedded in the Go binary — just access port 8080 directly.

webuiBasics.title: UI Overview
webuiBasics.desc: Get familiar with the WebUI layout, theme switching, and dashboard

layout.title: 1. Page Layout
layout.desc:
• Left sidebar: Dashboard, Sessions, Agents, Skills, Guide, Settings — six navigation entries, plus the 10 most recent sessions for quick access
• Top bar: Current page title, with theme toggle and language switch buttons on the right
• Center: Main content area showing the current page
• The sidebar can be collapsed/expanded via the top-left button; when collapsed, only icons are shown
layout.detail:
## Page Layout Structure

The interface uses a classic three-column layout:

```
┌──────────┬──────────────────────────────────┐
│          │         Top Title Bar             │
│  Left    ├──────────────────────────────────┤
│  Sidebar │                                  │
│          │         Main Content Area         │
│  • Dash  │                                  │
│  • Sess  │                                  │
│  • Agent │                                  │
│  • Skill │                                  │
│  • Guide │                                  │
│  • Setup │                                  │
│          │                                  │
│ ──────── │                                  │
│ Recent   │                                  │
└──────────┴──────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Left Sidebar

Six main navigation entries:

| Entry | Function |
|-------|----------|
| Dashboard | System status overview, agent and skill lists |
| Sessions | Create/manage multiple chat sessions |
| Agents | View registered agents and tools |
| Skills | Browse/search available skills |
| Guide | This page |
| Settings | Theme, language, connection info |

Below the nav entries, the 10 most recent sessions are shown for quick access.

### Collapse/Expand

Click the collapse button in the top-left corner to shrink the sidebar to icon-only mode, giving more space to the main content area.

themeAndLang.title: 2. Switch Theme & Language
themeAndLang.desc:
• Theme: Click the 🌙/☀️ icon in the top bar to toggle between light and dark mode. Dark mode uses a deep blue background (#0f172a) for eye comfort
• Language: Click the language icon to switch between Chinese and English. All UI text updates immediately
• On first visit, the system auto-detects your browser's preferences
themeAndLang.tip: Theme and language settings are saved in browser localStorage and automatically restored on refresh or revisit.
themeAndLang.detail:
## Theme Switching

Click the 🌙/☀️ icon in the top-right of the title bar to toggle between light and dark mode.

| Mode | Background | Best For |
|------|------------|----------|
| Light Mode | White/light gray | Daytime use |
| Dark Mode | Deep blue (#0f172a) | Night use, eye comfort |

## Language Switching

Click the language icon in the top bar to switch between Chinese and English:

- 🇨🇳 中文 — All UI text switches to Chinese
- 🇬🇧 English — All UI text switches to English

All text updates **instantly** — no page refresh needed.

## Auto Detection

On first visit, the system auto-detects your browser's language and theme preferences and applies them.

> Theme and language settings are saved in browser `localStorage` and automatically restored on refresh or revisit.

dashboard.title: 3. Dashboard Details
dashboard.desc:
The dashboard (home page) shows four key metrics:
• System Status: Green "Operational" or red "Offline"
• Active Sessions: Number of created sessions
• Registered Agents: Number of loaded agents (including CodeReview, Research sub-agents)
• Available Skills: Number of registered skills
Below are two lists showing all agents and skills in detail.
dashboard.detail:
## Dashboard Metrics

The dashboard (home page) displays four key metric cards:

| Metric | Description | Example |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| System Status | Backend service connection | 🟢 Operational / 🔴 Offline |
| Active Sessions | Number of created sessions | 3 |
| Registered Agents | Number of loaded agents | 3 (including sub-agents) |
| Available Skills | Number of registered skills | 5 |

## Agent List

Below the metrics, all registered agents are displayed:

- **Master Agent** — Main agent for task planning and execution
- **CodeReview Agent** — Code review sub-agent
- **Research Agent** — Information research sub-agent

## Skill List

Shows all available skills with name and description. Click to view details.

> If system status shows "Offline", check if the backend service is running and the API address is correctly configured.

sessionManagement.title: Session Management
sessionManagement.desc: Create, switch, and manage multiple independent sessions with isolated context

createSession.title: 1. Create a New Session
createSession.desc:
Two ways to create a session:
• Quick: Click the "Create" button in the left sidebar — auto-creates and navigates to the chat page
• Full: Go to "Sessions" page, click "New Session", enter a name (e.g. "Bug Fix", "Code Review"), optionally select a Profile (builder/reviewer/planner), and click Create

Each session has independent chat history and context — they don't interfere with each other.
createSession.tip: Sessions are persisted as Markdown files in ~/.claw/sessions/ and auto-restored on service restart.
createSession.detail:
## Quick Create

Click the **"+"** button at the bottom of the left sidebar:

1. System auto-creates a new session (with default name and Profile)
2. Auto-navigates to the chat page
3. Start typing messages immediately

## Full Create

1. Go to the **"Sessions"** page
2. Click **"New Session"** in the top-right
3. Fill in the form:
   - **Session name** (e.g. "Bug Fix", "Code Review", "Project Planning")
   - **Profile** (optional): Select builder/reviewer/planner, etc.
4. Click **"Create"**

## Session Isolation

Each session has **independent chat history and context** — they don't interfere with each other. You can maintain multiple sessions:

- One for daily development (builder)
- One for code reviews (reviewer)
- One for research (assistant)

> Sessions are persisted as Markdown files in `~/.claw/sessions/` and auto-restored on service restart.

sessionList.title: 2. Session List & Switching
sessionList.desc:
The "Sessions" page displays all sessions, each showing:
• Session name
• Message count
• Token usage
• Last active time

Click any session card to enter the chat page. You can also quickly switch using the "Recent Sessions" list in the left sidebar.
sessionList.detail:
## Session List

The "Sessions" page displays all sessions. Each card shows:

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Session name | The name specified at creation |
| Message count | Total messages in the session |
| Token usage | Tokens consumed |
| Last active | Time of last sent/received message |

## Switching Sessions

Two ways to quickly switch:

### Option 1: Sessions Page

Click any session card to enter its chat page.

### Option 2: Left Sidebar

The **"Recent Sessions"** list below the nav entries shows the 10 most recently active sessions. Click to jump directly.

> Sessions are sorted by last active time (most recent first).

deleteSession.title: 3. Delete & Clear Sessions
deleteSession.desc:
• Delete: In the session list, hover over a session card and click the delete button. After confirmation, the session and all messages are permanently deleted.
• Clear: In the chat page, click the "Clear" button at the top to erase all message history while keeping the session and Profile settings.
deleteSession.tip: Deletion is irreversible! Clearing only removes message history — the session itself is preserved.
deleteSession.detail:
## Delete a Session

1. Go to the **"Sessions"** page
2. Hover over the target session card
3. Click the 🗑️ **delete button** that appears
4. Click "Confirm" in the dialog

> ⚠️ Deletion is **irreversible**! The session and all messages are permanently removed.

## Clear Session Messages

1. Enter the target session's **chat page**
2. Click the **"Clear"** button at the top
3. Click "Confirm" in the dialog

## Delete vs Clear

| Action | Deletes Messages | Deletes Session | Keeps Profile |
|--------|-----------------|-----------------|---------------|
| Delete Session | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Clear Messages | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ |

Clearing only removes message history — the session and Profile settings are preserved for continued use.

forkRevert.title: 4. Session Fork & Revert
forkRevert.desc:
Advanced session management features:

• Fork: Create a branch copy from the current session, preserving a specified number of message history. Useful for "what if" exploration scenarios
• Revert: Roll back the session to a specified message index, removing all subsequent messages. Useful for undoing wrong conversation directions
• Export: Export the complete session as JSON format
forkRevert.tip: Use CLI commands /session fork and /session revert, or REST API endpoints POST /sessions/{id}/fork and POST /sessions/{id}/revert.
forkRevert.detail:
## Fork (Session Branching)

Create a branch copy from the current session:

### CLI Usage

```bash
/session fork [name] [fork_point]
```

- `name` — Branch name (optional, defaults to `original-name-fork`)
- `fork_point` — Fork from which message (optional, defaults to all messages)

### REST API

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions/{id}/fork \
  -d '{"name": "Explore Plan B", "fork_point": 10}'
```

## Revert (Session Rollback)

Roll back the session to a specified message index:

### CLI Usage

```bash
/session revert <message_index>
```

### REST API

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions/{id}/revert \
  -d '{"target_index": 5}'
```

## Export (Session Export)

Export the complete session as JSON:

### CLI Usage

```bash
/session export
```

> Forked branches are independent sessions — modifications don't affect the original session.

chatWithAgent.title: Chat with Agent
chatWithAgent.desc: Learn about messaging, agent states, action buttons, and the Canvas panel

sendMessage.title: 1. Sending Messages & Profile Switching
sendMessage.desc:
Type in the input box at the bottom of the chat page and press Enter to send.

• Normal chat: Just type your question, e.g. "Analyze the structure of main.go"
• Switch Profile: Prefix with @profile to change agent mode:
  - @direct Who are you? — Quick Q&A mode (no tools)
  - @assistant Analyze auth.go — Assistant mode (read-only tools)
  - @builder Refactor auth module — Builder mode (all tools)
  - @reviewer Check code quality — Reviewer mode
  - @debugger Debug login failure — Debugger mode
sendMessage.tip: Use Shift+Enter for line breaks. The system auto-selects the best Profile based on your message content — manual selection is rarely needed.
sendMessage.detail:
## Sending Messages

Type in the input box at the bottom of the chat page. Press **Enter** to send. Use **Shift+Enter** for line breaks.

## Profile Switching

Prefix your message with `@profile` to switch agent modes:

```
@direct Who are you?
@assistant Analyze the structure of auth.go
@builder Refactor the auth module
@reviewer Check recent code changes
@debugger Debug the login failure
```

### Profile Reference

| Profile | Tools | Best For |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `@direct` | None | Quick Q&A, knowledge queries |
| `@assistant` | Read-only | Code analysis, explanation |
| `@builder` | All tools | Development, code changes (default) |
| `@reviewer` | Read-only + git | Code review |
| `@debugger` | read/grep/bash | Debugging |
| `@planner` | Read-only | Task planning |
| `@writer` | read/write/glob | Documentation |
| `@explainer` | Read-only | Code explanation |

> The system auto-selects the best Profile based on your message — manual selection is rarely needed.

responseStates.title: 2. Understanding Agent Response States
responseStates.desc:
The agent goes through these states while processing:

• 🤔 Thinking — Analyzing your request and planning
• 🔧 Calling Tools — Executing tools (reading files, running commands, searching code, etc.)
• ✅ Completed — Processing done, reply displayed

The agent has 6 built-in tools: read_file, write_file, edit (string replace), glob (find files), grep (search content), bash (run commands).
responseStates.detail:
## Agent Response States

The agent goes through these states while processing:

| State | Icon | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| Thinking | 🤔 | Analyzing request, planning execution |
| Calling Tools | 🔧 | Executing tools (reading files, running commands, etc.) |
| Completed | ✅ | Processing done, reply displayed |

## Built-in Tools

| Tool | Function | Permission |
|------|----------|------------|
| `read_file` | Read file contents | Auto-allowed |
| `write_file` | Write/create files | Requires confirmation |
| `edit` | Precise string replacement | Requires confirmation |
| `glob` | Find files by pattern | Auto-allowed |
| `grep` | Search file contents | Auto-allowed |
| `bash` | Execute shell commands | Requires confirmation |

> Tools that "require confirmation" trigger the HITL approval workflow — the agent pauses and waits for your approval before proceeding.

messageActions.title: 3. Message Action Buttons
messageActions.desc:
Hover over any message to reveal action buttons on the right:

• 📋 Copy — Copy the full message to clipboard
• 👍 Helpful / 👎 Not helpful — Rate the agent's response
• 🔄 Regenerate — Generate a new response for this message
• 🗑️ Delete — Delete a single message

Code blocks have a built-in "Copy code" button. Long code blocks can be expanded/collapsed.
messageActions.detail:
## Message Action Buttons

Hover over any message to reveal action buttons on the right:

| Button | Function | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| 📋 Copy | Copy full message | Copies as plain text to clipboard |
| 👍 Helpful | Positive feedback | Mark response as good quality |
| 👎 Not helpful | Negative feedback | Mark response as poor quality |
| 🔄 Regenerate | New response | Generate a new reply for this message |
| 🗑️ Delete | Delete message | Remove a single message (confirmation required) |

## Code Block Features

Code blocks returned by the agent have extra features:

- **Copy code** — One-click code copying
- **Expand/Collapse** — Long code blocks can be folded
- **Fullscreen** — View code in fullscreen mode
- **Wrap lines** — Toggle line wrapping

> Code blocks support syntax highlighting with automatic language detection.

canvas.title: 4. Using the Canvas Panel
canvas.desc:
When the agent returns code, JSON, HTML, or other structured content, click "Open in Canvas" in the message to view it in the right-side Canvas panel:

• Preview mode: HTML content rendered directly
• Code mode: View source with syntax highlighting
• Download: Save content as a file
• Copy All: Copy the entire content

Canvas supports multiple open items with tab switching.
canvas.tip: HTML preview in Canvas has scripts disabled by default. Click "Enable Scripts" in the panel to allow execution.
canvas.detail:
## Opening the Canvas Panel

When the agent returns code, JSON, HTML, or other structured content:

1. Find the **"Open in Canvas"** button in the message
2. Click to display the content in the right-side Canvas panel

## Canvas Features

| Mode | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Preview | HTML/Markdown content rendered directly |
| Code | View source with syntax highlighting |

### Action Buttons

- **Download** — Save content as a file (auto-detects file type)
- **Copy All** — One-click copy of the entire content
- **Close** — Close the current tab
- **Close All** — Close all tabs

## Multiple Tabs

Canvas supports multiple open items with tab switching at the top.

> HTML preview has scripts disabled by default for security. Click "Enable Scripts" in the panel to allow execution.

toolsAndSkills.title: Tools & Skills
toolsAndSkills.desc: Learn about built-in tools, the skill system, and HITL approval workflow

viewAgents.title: 1. Built-in Tools & Agent List
viewAgents.desc:
Go to the "Agents" page to see all registered agents. The system includes these built-in tools:

• read_file — Read file contents (auto-allowed)
• write_file — Write to files (requires confirmation)
• edit — Precise string replacement (requires confirmation)
• glob — Find files by pattern (auto-allowed)
• grep — Search file contents (auto-allowed)
• bash — Execute shell commands (requires confirmation)

The system also has CodeReview and Research sub-agents for code review and information research.
viewAgents.tip: Tool permissions have three levels: Allow (auto-allowed), Ask (requires HITL confirmation), Deny (auto-rejected). Customize in hitl.permission_rules in the config file.
viewAgents.detail:
## View Agent List

Go to the **"Agents"** page to see all registered agents.

## Built-in Tool Permissions

| Tool | Description | Default Permission |
|------|-------------|-------------------|
| `read_file` | Read file contents at a given path | ✅ Allow (auto-allowed) |
| `write_file` | Write content to a specified file | ⚠️ Ask (requires confirmation) |
| `edit` | Perform precise string replacement in files | ⚠️ Ask (requires confirmation) |
| `glob` | Find files matching a glob pattern | ✅ Allow (auto-allowed) |
| `grep` | Search file contents with regex | ✅ Allow (auto-allowed) |
| `bash` | Execute shell commands and return output | ⚠️ Ask (requires confirmation) |

## Sub-agents

The system includes two specialized sub-agents:

- **CodeReview Agent** — Focuses on code review, uses read-only tools to analyze quality
- **Research Agent** — Focuses on information research, searches and summarizes relevant materials

> Tool permissions can be customized in `hitl.permission_rules` in the config. Set to `allow` to skip confirmation, `deny` to block entirely.

allTools.title: Complete Tool List
allTools.desc:
The system registers 22+ tools, grouped by category:

Core tools: read_file, write_file, edit, glob, grep, bash, ls, multiedit, apply_patch
Web tools: websearch, webfetch
Batch & parallel: batch, parallel_dispatch
Interaction & tasks: question, task, skill
State management: todoread, todowrite
IM communication: send_im_message
LSP language services: hover, definition, references, etc. (9 total)
WeChat operations: wechat_rich_message, etc. (6 total)
allTools.detail:
## Complete Tool List

The system registers 22+ tools, grouped by function:

### Core File Operations

| Tool | Description | Default Permission |
|------|-------------|-------------------|
| `read_file` | Read file contents | ✅ Allow |
| `write_file` | Write/create files | ⚠️ Ask |
| `edit` | Precise string replacement editing | ⚠️ Ask |
| `multiedit` | Batch multi-file editing | ⚠️ Ask |
| `apply_patch` | Apply unified diff patches | ⚠️ Ask |
| `glob` | Find files by pattern | ✅ Allow |
| `grep` | Search file contents | ✅ Allow |
| `ls` | List directory contents | ✅ Allow |
| `bash` | Execute shell commands | ⚠️ Ask |

### Web Tools

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `websearch` | Web search |
| `webfetch` | Fetch web page content |

### Batch & Parallel

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `batch` | Batch execute multiple tool calls |
| `parallel_dispatch` | Dispatch tasks to multiple SubAgents in parallel |

### Interaction & Tasks

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `question` | Ask user for input |
| `task` | Create and manage subtasks |
| `skill` | Invoke registered skills |

### State Management

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `todoread` | Read todo list |
| `todowrite` | Update todo list |

### IM Communication

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `send_im_message` | Send messages to IM platforms |

### LSP Language Services

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `hover` | Hover info (type/docs) |
| `definition` | Go to definition |
| `references` | Find all references |
| `implementation` | Go to implementation |
| `document_symbol` | Document symbol list |
| `workspace_symbol` | Workspace symbol search |
| `call_hierarchy` | Call hierarchy |
| `incoming_calls` | Who calls this function |
| `outgoing_calls` | What this function calls |

### WeChat Operation Tools (requires WeChatPadPro)

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `wechat_rich_message` | Send rich text messages |
| `wechat_contact` | Contact management |
| `wechat_group` | Group management |
| `wechat_moments` | Moments operations |
| `wechat_user` | User info queries |
| `wechat_admin` | Admin operations |

viewSkills.title: 2. Using the Skill System
viewSkills.desc:
Go to "Skills" to browse available skills. Skills are declarative instruction/knowledge packs (Markdown files), distinct from executable Tools.

Create a custom Skill: Create a subdirectory under .claude/skills/ with a SKILL.md file:

---
name: python-linter
description: Python code checking guide
allowed-tools: ["read_file", "bash"]
---
Check code following PEP 8 standards using pylint...

Skill search paths: .claude/skills/ (project) → ~/.claude/skills/ (user) → skills/ (built-in)
viewSkills.detail:
## Browse Skills

Go to the **"Skills"** page to browse all available skills. Skills are declarative instruction/knowledge packs (Markdown files), distinct from executable Tools.

## Create a Custom Skill

Create a subdirectory under `.claude/skills/` with a `SKILL.md` file:

```markdown
---
name: python-linter
description: Python code checking guide
allowed-tools: ["read_file", "bash"]
---

## Checking Standards

Follow PEP 8 standards using these tools:

1. Use `read_file` to read the target file
2. Use `bash` to run `pylint`
3. Summarize results and provide fix suggestions
```

## Skill Search Paths

Skills are searched in this order:

| Priority | Path | Scope |
|----------|------|-------|
| 1 (highest) | `.claude/skills/` | Project-level |
| 2 | `~/.claude/skills/` | User-level |
| 3 (lowest) | `skills/` | Built-in |

> Same-name skills are overridden by priority: project > user > built-in.

hitl.title: 3. Human Approval (HITL) Workflow
hitl.desc:
When the agent performs dangerous operations like writing files or running shell commands, the system pauses and shows an approval panel:

• Permission Request: Shows operation details (e.g. "Execute command: rm -rf /tmp/test")
• Approve: Confirm the operation
• Reject: Block it — the agent will try alternatives
• Provide Input: Some scenarios require your additional information

Approval requests appear in the notification area at the top and the sidebar shows pending count.
hitl.tip: Customize approval rules in config. For example, setting bash tool's action to "allow" skips command approval (not recommended for production). Default timeout is 5 minutes, configurable via hitl.input_timeout.
hitl.detail:
## HITL Approval Workflow

When the agent performs an operation requiring confirmation, the system pauses and shows an approval panel:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ⚠️ Permission Request              │
│                                     │
│  The agent requests to:             │
│  Execute command: rm -rf /tmp/test  │
│                                     │
│  [Approve]  [Reject]  [Provide Input]│
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Approval Options

| Button | Action | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Approve | Confirm | Agent proceeds with the operation |
| Reject | Block | Agent tries alternative approaches |
| Provide Input | Add info | Give the agent additional guidance |

## Notifications

- **Notification area** at the top shows pending approvals
- Left sidebar shows **pending count** badge
- WebSocket real-time push supported

## Timeout Configuration

Default timeout is **5 minutes**. Customize in config:

```json
{
  "hitl": {
    "input_timeout": "10m"
  }
}
```

> Customize approval rules in config. Setting bash tool's action to `allow` skips command approval (not recommended for production).

parallelTasks.title: 4. Parallel Tasks & Progress Tracking
parallelTasks.desc:
The system supports decomposing complex tasks into multiple subtasks executed in parallel, with real-time progress display:

• parallel_dispatch tool: Distributes tasks to multiple SubAgents for concurrent processing
• Real-time progress panel: Shows task group progress, subtask status, and Agent tool calls in the chat interface
parallelTasks.detail:
## Parallel Task System

### Parallel Dispatch

The Agent executes multiple subtasks simultaneously via `parallel_dispatch`, collects all subtask results, and generates the final reply.

### Progress Tracking Panel

The progress panel in the chat interface shows in real-time:

| Info | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Task group status | started → completed/failed |
| Subtask list | Each task's ID, description, and current status |
| Agent tool calls | Tools being used by each SubAgent and turn count |

### Event Types

The system broadcasts the following events via WebSocket:

- `task_group` — Parallel task group lifecycle
- `task_progress` — Individual task progress
- `agent_progress` — SubAgent tool-call level progress

configuration.title: Configuration
configuration.desc: Configure system behavior via config.json and environment variables

configFile.title: 1. Create Config File
configFile.tip: All settings have sensible defaults. For first use, you only need to set the LLM API key.
configFile.detail:
## Create Config File

Create `config.json` in the project root:

```json
{
  "server": {
    "port": 8080,
    "host": "0.0.0.0",
    "cors_origins": ["http://localhost:3000"]
  },
  "logging": {
    "level": "info",
    "format": "json"
  },
  "hitl": {
    "enabled": true,
    "input_timeout": "5m",
    "websocket_enabled": true
  },
  "agent": {
    "timeout": "30m"
  }
}
```

## Configuration Reference

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| `server.port` | `8080` | HTTP server port |
| `server.host` | `0.0.0.0` | Listen address |
| `server.cors_origins` | `["*"]` | Allowed CORS origins |
| `logging.level` | `info` | Log level (debug/info/warn/error) |
| `logging.format` | `json` | Log format (json/text) |
| `hitl.enabled` | `true` | Enable HITL approval |
| `hitl.input_timeout` | `5m` | Approval timeout |
| `agent.timeout` | `30m` | Single task timeout |

## Starting with Config

```bash
./server --config config.json
```

> All settings have sensible defaults. For first use, you only need to set the LLM API key.

llmProvider.title: 2. Configure LLM Provider
llmProvider.desc:
In the llm section of config.json:

{
  "llm": {
    "provider": "openai",
    "api_key": "sk-...",
    "model": "gpt-4",
    "base_url": "https://www.gmini.xyz/v1"
  }
}

Supported providers:
• openai — default model gpt-4o
• deepseek — default model deepseek-chat
• anthropic — default model claude-3-5-sonnet
• custom — any OpenAI-compatible API

Env vars also work: CLAW_API_KEY, CLAW_MODEL, CLAW_BASE_URL, CLAW_PROVIDER (higher priority than config file).
llmProvider.tip: Support multiple models — add more in the llm.models array and switch at runtime with the /model command.
llmProvider.detail:
## LLM Configuration

In the `llm` section of `config.json`:

```json
{
  "llm": {
    "provider": "openai",
    "api_key": "sk-...",
    "model": "gpt-4o",
    "base_url": "https://www.gmini.xyz/v1"
  }
}
```

## Supported Providers

| Provider | Value | Default Model | Base URL |
|----------|-------|---------------|----------|
| OpenAI | `openai` | `gpt-4o` | `https://www.gmini.xyz/v1` |
| DeepSeek | `deepseek` | `deepseek-chat` | `https://api.deepseek.com/v1` |
| Anthropic | `anthropic` | `claude-3-5-sonnet` | `https://api.anthropic.com/v1` |
| Custom | `custom` | Must specify | Must specify |

## Environment Variables

Environment variables take priority over config file:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `CLAW_API_KEY` | API Key (highest priority) |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI API Key |
| `CLAW_MODEL` | Model name |
| `CLAW_BASE_URL` | API base URL |
| `CLAW_PROVIDER` | Provider name |

## Multi-model Configuration

```json
{
  "llm": {
    "provider": "openai",
    "api_key": "sk-...",
    "model": "gpt-4o",
    "models": [
      { "name": "gpt-4o", "provider": "openai" },
      { "name": "deepseek-chat", "provider": "deepseek", "base_url": "https://api.deepseek.com/v1" }
    ]
  }
}
```

> Switch models at runtime with the `/model` command.

profiles.title: 3. Profile System Setup
profiles.desc:
10 built-in Profiles with different tool permissions:

• direct — No tools, quick Q&A
• assistant — read/glob/grep, read-only
• builder — All tools (default)
• planner — Read-only, task planning
• reviewer — Read-only + git, code review
• debugger — read/grep/bash/git, debugging
• writer — read/write/glob, documentation
• explainer — Read-only, code explanation
• im-operator — IM tools + read-only, IM message management
• researcher — websearch/webfetch + read-only, information research

Custom Profiles: Create ~/.claw/profiles.json with custom name, description, tool permissions, and system prompts.
profiles.detail:
## Built-in Profiles

10 pre-configured profiles covering common use cases:

| Profile | Tools | Best For |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `direct` | None | Quick Q&A |
| `assistant` | read/glob/grep | Code analysis |
| `builder` | All tools | Development (default) |
| `planner` | Read-only | Task planning |
| `reviewer` | Read-only + git | Code review |
| `debugger` | read/grep/bash/git | Debugging |
| `writer` | read/write/glob | Documentation |
| `explainer` | Read-only | Code explanation |
| `im-operator` | IM tools + read-only | IM message management |
| `researcher` | websearch/webfetch + read-only | Information research |

## Custom Profiles

Create `~/.claw/profiles.json`:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "security-auditor",
    "description": "Security audit profile",
    "tools": ["read_file", "glob", "grep", "bash"],
    "system_prompt": "You are a security audit expert focused on finding vulnerabilities..."
  }
]
```

Usage:

```
@security-auditor Check auth.go for security issues
```

> Custom profiles merge with built-in ones. Same-name custom profiles take priority.

extensionsPlugins.title: 4. Extensions & Plugins
extensionsPlugins.desc:
The system supports multiple extension methods:

• Plugin system: Place JSON-RPC plugins in the plugin.dir directory, auto-loaded by the system
• MCP servers: Configure external MCP servers for additional tools (supports stdio/SSE/HTTP transport)
• Custom tools: Place tool definition files in the custom_tools_dir directory
• Custom commands: Configure shortcut command templates in the commands section of config.json

imChannels.title: IM Integration
imChannels.desc: Connect DingTalk, Feishu, WeCom, or personal WeChat to chat with agents in IM

overview.title: 1. Supported IM Platforms & Architecture
overview.desc:
The Channel system bridges IM messages to Agent sessions:

IM Platform → Webhook → Channel Router → ACP Control Plane → Master Agent

4 platforms supported:
• DingTalk (dingtalk) — Enterprise, requires DingTalk app creation
• Feishu (feishu) — Enterprise, requires Feishu app creation
• WeCom (wecom) — Enterprise, requires WeCom admin access
• Personal WeChat (wechat) — 4 protocol backends (openwechat / gewechat / wcferry / wechaty)

Message types: direct (DM), group (group chat), channel.
overview.detail:
## Architecture Flow

```
IM User Message
    ↓
Webhook Endpoint (/api/v1/webhook/{platform})
    ↓
Channel Router (lookup Binding mapping)
    ↓
ACP Control Plane (rate limiting, auth)
    ↓
Master Agent (process and reply)
    ↓
IM Reply Message
```

## Supported Platforms

| Platform | ID | Type | Requirements |
|----------|-----|------|-------------|
| DingTalk | `dingtalk` | Enterprise | Create DingTalk app |
| Feishu | `feishu` | Enterprise | Create Feishu app |
| WeCom | `wecom` | Enterprise | WeCom admin access |
| Personal WeChat | `wechat` | Personal | Choose protocol backend |

## Message Types

| Type | ID | Description |
|------|-----|-------------|
| Direct | `direct` | One-on-one private messages |
| Group | `group` | Group chat messages |
| Channel | `channel` | Channel/topic messages |

> Personal WeChat supports 4 protocol backends (openwechat / gewechat / wcferry / wechaty), each with different compatibility and stability characteristics.

channelConfig.title: 2. Platform Configuration
channelConfig.desc:
Enable and configure platforms in the channel section of config.json:

DingTalk: requires app_key, app_secret, callback token, aes_key, and agent_id
Feishu: requires app_id, app_secret, verification_token, and encrypt_key
WeCom: requires corp_id, agent_id, secret, callback token, and encoding_aes_key

Personal WeChat has 4 protocols:
• openwechat — Web protocol, enable uos:true for desktop mode login
• gewechat — iPad protocol (HTTP API), requires paid service
• wcferry — PC Hook (NNG communication), requires Windows PC with injector
• wechaty — gRPC bridge, requires Puppet Token
channelConfig.tip: Personal WeChat integration uses unofficial protocols with account ban risks. Use a test account, not your primary one.

webhook.title: 3. Webhook & Message Routing
webhook.desc:
Configure the IM platform's webhook callback URL:

• DingTalk/Feishu/WeCom: In the platform's developer console, set event callback URL to http://your-host:8080/api/v1/webhook/{platform}
• gewechat: Set callback_url to http://your-host:8080/api/v1/webhook/wechat

Message routing flow:
1. IM message arrives at webhook endpoint
2. Router looks up Binding (platform:chatID → sessionID mapping)
3. ACP Control Plane performs rate limiting
4. Master Agent processes message and replies

Use Gateway RPC's channel.bind method to manually bind an IM channel to a specific session.
webhook.tip: Ensure the webhook port is publicly accessible by the IM platform. HTTPS with nginx reverse proxy is recommended.
webhook.detail:
## Webhook URL Format

Callback URLs for each platform:

| Platform | Webhook URL |
|----------|-------------|
| DingTalk | `http://your-host:8080/api/v1/webhook/dingtalk` |
| Feishu | `http://your-host:8080/api/v1/webhook/feishu` |
| WeCom | `http://your-host:8080/api/v1/webhook/wecom` |
| WeChat (gewechat) | `http://your-host:8080/api/v1/webhook/wechat` |

## Setup Steps

1. Go to the IM platform's **developer console**
2. Find the event callback configuration
3. Set the callback URL to the format above
4. Enter the verification token and encryption key (matching config.json)
5. Save and verify the connection

## Message Routing Flow

1. IM message arrives at the webhook endpoint
2. Router looks up **Binding** (`platform:chatID` → `sessionID` mapping)
3. ACP Control Plane performs **rate limiting**
4. Master Agent **processes and replies**

## Manual Channel Binding

Bind an IM channel to a session via Gateway RPC:

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/rpc \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "method": "channel.bind",
    "params": {
      "platform": "dingtalk",
      "chat_id": "group-123",
      "session_id": "sess-456"
    }
  }'
```

> Ensure the webhook port is publicly accessible by the IM platform. HTTPS with nginx reverse proxy is recommended.

apiReference.title: API Reference
apiReference.desc: HTTP REST API and WebSocket interface usage

healthAndSession.title: 1. Session Management API
healthAndSession.desc:
Base URL: http://localhost:8080/api/v1

• GET /health — Check service status, returns {"status":"ok"}
• POST /sessions — Create session, Body: {"name":"my-session", "profile":"builder"}, returns session_id
• GET /sessions — List all sessions
• GET /sessions/{id} — Get session details
• PATCH /sessions/{id} — Update session (e.g. rename)
• DELETE /sessions/{id} — Delete session
• POST /sessions/{id}/clear — Clear message history
healthAndSession.detail:
## Base Info

- **Base URL**: `http://localhost:8080/api/v1`
- **Content-Type**: `application/json`

## Health Check

```bash
curl http://localhost:8080/api/v1/health
```

Response:

```json
{
  "status": "ok"
}
```

## Session APIs

### Create Session

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "my-session", "profile": "builder"}'
```

Response:

```json
{
  "id": "sess-abc123",
  "name": "my-session",
  "profile": "builder",
  "created_at": "2026-03-16T10:00:00Z"
}
```

### List All Sessions

```bash
curl http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions
```

### Get Session Details

```bash
curl http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions/{id}
```

### Update Session

```bash
curl -X PATCH http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions/{id} \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "new-name"}'
```

### Delete Session

```bash
curl -X DELETE http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions/{id}
```

### Clear Message History

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions/{id}/clear
```

chatApi.title: 2. Chat & HITL API
chatApi.desc:
• POST /sessions/{id}/messages — Send message, Body: {"content":"Analyze main.go"}, returns agent reply

HITL endpoints:
• GET /tasks/{id}/pending-input — Query pending approval requests
• POST /tasks/{id}/input — Submit approval response, Body: {"input_id":"xxx", "response":"yes"}
• POST /tasks/{id}/command — Send control command (e.g. "cancel")

Other APIs:
• GET /agents — List all agents
• GET /skills — List all skills
chatApi.tip: There's also a Gateway RPC interface (POST /api/v1/rpc) using JSON-RPC 2.0, with token auth and more operations like channel.send, plugin.list, etc.
chatApi.detail:
## Send Message

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/sessions/{id}/messages \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"content": "Analyze the structure of main.go"}'
```

Response:

```json
{
  "id": "msg-xyz789",
  "role": "assistant",
  "content": "The structure of main.go is...",
  "created_at": "2026-03-16T10:01:00Z"
}
```

## HITL API

### Query Pending Approvals

```bash
curl http://localhost:8080/api/v1/tasks/{id}/pending-input
```

### Submit Approval Response

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/tasks/{id}/input \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"input_id": "inp-001", "response": "yes"}'
```

### Cancel Task

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/tasks/{id}/command \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"command": "cancel"}'
```

## Other APIs

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `/agents` | GET | List all agents |
| `/skills` | GET | List all skills |

> There's also a Gateway RPC interface (`POST /api/v1/rpc`) using JSON-RPC 2.0 with token auth and more operations.

websocket.title: 3. WebSocket Real-time Communication
websocket.desc:
Connect to ws://localhost:8080/api/v1/ws for a real-time channel.

WebSocket is used for:
• Streaming agent responses (token by token)
• Agent state changes (thinking → tool_calling → completed)
• HITL approval request push notifications
• Real-time message notifications

Requires config: hitl.websocket_enabled: true

The frontend WebSocket client has built-in auto-reconnect on disconnect.
websocket.tip: The Gateway also provides a WebSocket RPC endpoint at /api/v1/rpc/ws with Bearer token auth, supporting all RPC methods in real-time.

remoteAgents.title: Remote Agents
remoteAgents.subtitle: Manage remote Agents connected via ACP protocol
remoteAgents.noAgentsHint: Click "Add Agent" below to connect a remote ACP Agent
remoteAgents.name: Name
remoteAgents.descriptionPlaceholder: Agent capability description for Planner reference
remoteAgents.commandRequired: Command is required for stdio transport
remoteAgents.urlRequired: URL is required for http transport

runtimeConfig.title: Runtime Config
runtimeConfig.permissionRulesHint: Rules are matched in order; the first match wins. Tool names support wildcard *
runtimeConfig.execRulesHint: Define regex patterns to allow, ask, or deny command execution. Rules are evaluated in order; first match wins.
runtimeConfig.mcpServersHint: Configure external MCP servers for additional tools. Changes require saving to file and restarting the service.
runtimeConfig.mcpNeedRestart: MCP changes require saving to file and restart
runtimeConfig.imChannelsHint: Configure DingTalk, Feishu, and WeCom integration. Changes require saving to file and restarting the service.
runtimeConfig.feishuAckEmojiHint: Emoji the bot reacts with on message receipt (Feishu emoji_type, CamelCase). Get (default 👍) / Typing (⌨️) / disabled.
runtimeConfig.feishuIngressModeHint: webhook needs public HTTPS endpoint at /api/v1/channel/feishu/webhook; longconn doesn't need public IP, bot dials ws.open.feishu.cn:443. CEO ruling: webhook XOR longconn, never both in same process.
runtimeConfig.feishuGapFetchEnabledHint: After longconn reconnect, fetch missed messages during disconnection. Default off.
runtimeConfig.feishuWebhookURLHint: Public URL declaring this process serves webhook (optional, just a declaration; the real config is in Feishu console). Non-empty + longconn=true = startup panic.
runtimeConfig.feishuRegionHint: cn = open.feishu.cn (mainland Feishu); intl = open.larksuite.com (overseas Lark). SDK base URL switches accordingly.
runtimeConfig.feishuReloadHint: After saving config, click here to apply new config to running plugin (no restart needed)
runtimeConfig.feishuStreamingHint: When enabled, agent responses patch the same Feishu card incrementally via event stream. When disabled, falls back to legacy one-shot plain-text reply.
runtimeConfig.feishuThrottleHint: Minimum interval between card updates, default 300ms. Too low hits Feishu rate limits; too high increases perceived latency.
runtimeConfig.feishuShowAgentProgressHint: Display intermediate states like "Agent thinking..." in the card. Default off.
runtimeConfig.channelNeedRestart: IM channel changes require saving to file and restart
runtimeConfig.wechatDesc: Configure personal WeChat integration protocol (WeChatPadPro recommended)
runtimeConfig.wechatyDesc: gRPC-based WeChat bridge, requires a separate Wechaty service
runtimeConfig.wechatTip1: WeChatPadPro: Recommended — requires Docker or WeChatPadPro service
runtimeConfig.wechatTip2: Wechaty: Alternative — requires a separate Wechaty Puppet service
runtimeConfig.wechatTip3: After changing config, click Save, then Reload to apply
runtimeConfig.wechatTip4: First launch requires QR code scan; login is saved automatically

canvas.noPreview: Preview not available for this type

externalResources.title: External Resources
externalResources.description: Configure connections to databases, monitoring, APIs and other external systems. Agents use these automatically when needed.
